content: Step 1 (Route, agent-router shape classification): none of the 3 requested items are a new command, sub-agent, or domain plugin -- they're root-level convention/infrastructure files (peers of the existing AGENTS.md, COLONY.md, .agents/rules/). Classify as 'cross-cutting Colony infrastructure', closest precedent is the EC-010 doc-staleness cycle (ceo/e5e3107f) which edited root docs directly rather than routing through skill-creator packaging. Do not force-fit skill-creator.

Step 2 (Dispatch -- cto worker): architecture-fit review of each item against this repo's actual documented toolchain (AGENTS.md states Claude Code + Codex CLI only; no Cline anywhere in this repo). Item 1 (.clinerules / Cline Memory Bank) is a third tool's convention with zero existing footprint here, and this repo already has its own persistence layer (.pmcro/trails) -- flag as a likely scope/architecture mismatch rather than silently building it. Items 2 and 3 fit cleanly: CLAUDE.md is Claude Code's own root-convention file, structurally the same twin relationship AGENTS.md already has to Codex (same pattern EC-011 formalized for the two marketplace.json files).

Step 2 (Dispatch -- clo worker): Law 4 domain-boundary check. None of the 3 items belong to a single C-Suite domain's Owns/Does-Not-Own scope -- they're Colony-wide governance, same category as AGENTS.md/COLONY.md, which ceo has previously edited directly (via reflector, e.g. EC-010) without another domain's sign-off. No cross-domain violation. Separately flag: item 1's stated purpose (session persistence) risks duplicating/competing with the trail system (.pmcro/trails) that already IS this Colony's session-persistence mechanism -- a boundary concern between 'a new memory system' and 'the one this Colony already has', not a C-Suite domain boundary concern.

Draft CLAUDE.md content: point at catalog/skills.json and the 10 domain plugin descriptions already in .agents/plugins/marketplace.json as the single source of truth for the routing table, rather than hand-listing every command inline -- hand-listing would create exactly the drift EC-010 and EC-011 both already had to fix once. Draft the Startup Protocol as an addition to both AGENTS.md and the new CLAUDE.md (kept identical in both, twin-style, per EC-011's precedent) rather than living in only one.

Per evolve-colony.md step 4: seal needs-approval unconditionally regardless of check outcome. Stage all proposed content as .md documents inside this trail; write nothing to a live root file. Success condition: git status shows zero modified/new files outside .pmcro/trails/ceo/4cf2171d... after this cycle.
